message:
feat(iconv): expand iconv codec implementation and add scope ledger conformance

Extends the iconv module with 43 new lines of codec handling logic,
updates the feature parity documentation and support matrix to reflect
the expanded iconv coverage, and adds a new iconv_codec_scope_ledger
conformance test with its corresponding v1 JSON fixture. Also syncs
the beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Structured logs for coverage snapshots and blocker reasons.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyWaterfall","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:39.404348725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:10:45.753331316Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:10:45.753260964Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","reporting","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":172,"issue_id":"bd-3cco","author":"SnowyWaterfall","text":"Validation evidence: 1) python3 -m py_compile scripts/generate_test_obligation_dashboard.py 2) shellcheck scripts/check_test_obligation_dashboard.sh 3) TMPDIR=/data/tmp scripts/check_test_obligation_dashboard.sh 4) TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test test_obligation_dashboard_test -- --nocapture (PASS).  {"id":"bd-3dv","title":"Perf: Hard rule audit (no exp/ln/matrix solve on strict fast path)","description":"Audit and enforce that expensive math is not executed on the strict fast path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Identify any use of exp/ln/div-heavy float ops or linear algebra in decide() fast path.
- Move such work to cadenced resample (e.g. every 64/256 calls) and cache results.
- Provide before/after microbench evidence.

Rationale:
- Strict mode budget is ~20ns; a single exp() can exceed it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownMill","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.625369194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:26:49.466951862Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:26:21.830071851Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dv","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:13.900966129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-3dv","author":"BrownMill","text":"Taking over bd-3dv (previously assignee BlueLake).  {"id":"bd-3een","title":"LCRQ ring queues for IPC (section 14.6)","description":"Implement LCRQ (Linked Concurrent Ring Queue) for high-performance inter-process communication in FrankenLibC. LCRQ combines the cache-friendliness of ring buffers with the unbounded capacity of linked lists. Structure: a linked list of fixed-size ring segments. Each segment is a cache-line-aligned array with head/tail indices using FAA (Fetch-And-Add) for O(1) enqueue/dequeue. When a segment fills, a new one is linked. Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Evidence ledger IPC — when the evidence ledger runs in a separate process (for crash isolation), LCRQ provides the high-throughput, low-latency communication channel. Target: >10M events/sec with <100ns per-event latency. (2) Deferred free IPC — in hardened mode, freed blocks may be sent to a separate quarantine manager process. LCRQ handles the transfer without blocking the application thread. (3) Metric export — real-time allocation statistics exported to monitoring via LCRQ. Implementation: (a) LcrqSegment<T, N> — ring buffer of N slots. (b) Lcrq<T, N> — linked list of segments with hazard-pointer-based reclamation for consumed segments. (c) Shared memory support — LCRQ must work across processes via mmap'd shared memory (all pointers must be offset-based, not absolute). (d) Backpressure — when the consumer falls behind, the producer sees a full queue and applies configurable backpressure (block, drop, or signal).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.6 of graveyard. LCRQ from Morrison & Afek (2013 PPoPP). Linked ring queue combining CRQ segments for unbounded MPMC queues. Hazard pointer reclamation from Michael (2004).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- LcrqSegment<T, const N: usize>: ring buffer of N slots, head/tail as AtomicU64 using fetch_add.
- Lcrq<T, const N: usize>: linked list of segments. New segment allocated when current fills.
- Slot: enum { Empty, Full(T), Invalid }. CAS-based enqueue/dequeue on individual slots.
- Shared memory: offset-based pointers (usize offsets from base) for cross-process use via mmap.
- Hazard pointer: reclaim consumed segments after grace period (integrate with RCU from bd-3aof if available).
- Backpressure: BackpressurePolicy enum { Block, Drop, Signal } configurable per queue instance.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. LCRQ passes MPMC correctness test: 16 producers + 16 consumers, 1M items, zero lost items.
2. Throughput >10M items/sec for 64-byte payloads on single-socket system.
3. Per-item latency <100ns at p99 under moderate load (50% utilization).
4. Shared memory mode: cross-process producer/consumer works correctly (test with fork()).
5. Backpressure: Block mode blocks producer when consumer falls behind, Drop mode drops with counter, Signal mode sends SIGUSR2.
6. Segment reclamation: no memory leak after 10M enqueue/dequeue cycles.
7. Miri clean for single-threaded correctness path.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: lcrq, operation=enqueue|dequeue, segment_id, slot_index, backpressure) per operation.
- tracing::debug!(target: lcrq, segment_allocated|segment_reclaimed, segment_id, total_segments) on segment lifecycle.
- Periodic: queue_depth, throughput_ops_per_sec, backpressure_events via tracing::info every 100K ops.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:19.541620688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:44.866650722Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3ex","title":"Gate: workspace builds + clippy clean + abi cdylib builds","description":"Critique mapping: #1.

Goal: prove the repo compiles as a complete unit.

Deliverables:
- One canonical gate command that runs:
  - cargo fmt --check
  - cargo check --workspace --all-targets
  - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
  - cargo test --workspace
  - cargo build -p glibc-rs-abi --release (cdylib)

Acceptance:
- Gate passes on a clean checkout.
- Gate fails fast and loudly on any warning, missing cfg, or target mismatch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoEagle","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:26.593924925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:42:20.428059146Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:52:57.215408Z","close_reason":"Canonical workspace gate implemented and passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","e2e"],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-3ex","author":"IndigoEagle","text":"Implemented canonical gate in scripts/ci.sh per bead acceptance: fmt/check(workspace+all-targets)/clippy(workspace+all-targets, -D warnings)/test(workspace)/abi release build. Moved previous policy/perf/snapshot checks behind GLIBC_RUST_EXTENDED_GATES=1 opt-in. Updated README quick-start with canonical and extended commands. Verified scripts/ci.sh passes end-to-end.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:52:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3f6f","title":"CI: comprehensive pipeline integration","description":"Comprehensive CI pipeline for all quality gates.

Goal: Automated CI that validates all FrankenLibC quality gates.

CI Jobs:

1. Build Matrix
   - cargo build --release (all targets)
   - cargo build --release -p frankenlibc-abi
   - cargo build --features=standalone (L2+)

2. Quality Gates
   - cargo fmt --check
   - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
   - cargo test --all-targets

3. Conformance Suite
   - scripts/ci.sh (basic gate)
   - scripts/conformance_golden_gate.sh
   - scripts/snapshot_gate.sh

4. LD_PRELOAD Smoke
   - scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh (strict)
   - FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh

5. Performance Gates
   - cargo bench -p frankenlibc-bench
   - Strict <20ns validation
   - Hardened <200ns validation
   - No regressions vs baseline

6. CVE Arena
   - All CVE triggers pass in hardened mode
   - Metrics capture for evidence

7. Fuzzing (nightly)
   - 1M iterations per fuzz target
   - Crash reporting
   - Coverage report

8. Multi-arch (when available)
   - aarch64 cross-compile
   - QEMU test run

Artifacts:
- Build logs
- Test results (JUnit XML)
- Coverage reports
- Benchmark results
- Fuzzing coverage

Success Criteria:
- All gates pass on every PR
- Performance baselines maintained
- Coverage reports generated
- Nightly fuzzing runs

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-3fb","title":"EPIC: Extreme Optimization Loop (baselines + profiles + goldens)","description":"Enforce the extreme-software-optimization loop for allocator + membrane:
BASELINE -> PROFILE -> PROVE -> IMPLEMENT (one lever) -> VERIFY -> REPEAT.

Deliverables:
- Baseline suite (hyperfine + criterion).
- Flamegraph/profiler playbook.
- Golden outputs + isomorphism proof template wired into PR process.

Acceptance:
- We can prove behavior unchanged after optimizations via goldens/checksums.
- Perf regressions are detected quickly and reproducibly.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:07.189454921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:02.023072672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:02.023028389Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:40:06.776846377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-1ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:55.057923860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-22p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:55.269519020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-26o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.732163604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2bd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.945025655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2r0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:55.163975230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2wp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.622778174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-f7r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.845997280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -59,7 +59,7 @@ Source of truth: `tests/conformance/hard_parts_truth_table.v1.json` (generated `
 - `resolver`: `IMPLEMENTED_PARTIAL` — implemented scope: bootstrap numeric resolver ABI (`getaddrinfo`, `freeaddrinfo`, `getnameinfo`, `gai_strerror`). Deferred scope: full retry/cache/poisoning hardening campaign.
 - `nss`: `IMPLEMENTED_PARTIAL` — implemented scope: passwd/group APIs are exported as `Implemented` via `pwd_abi`/`grp_abi`. Deferred scope: hosts/backend breadth plus NSS concurrency/cache-coherence closure.
 - `locale`: `IMPLEMENTED_PARTIAL` — implemented scope: bootstrap `setlocale`/`localeconv` C/POSIX path. Deferred scope: catalog, collation, and transliteration parity expansion.
-- `iconv`: `IMPLEMENTED_PARTIAL` — implemented scope: phase-1 `iconv_open`/`iconv`/`iconv_close` conversions with deterministic strict+hardened fixtures. Deferred scope: full `iconvdata` breadth and deterministic table-generation closure.
+- `iconv`: `IMPLEMENTED_PARTIAL` — implemented scope: phase-1 `iconv_open`/`iconv`/`iconv_close` conversions for UTF-8/ISO-8859-1/UTF-16LE/UTF-32 with deterministic strict+hardened fixtures; codec scope/exclusions are locked in `tests/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.v1.json`. Deferred scope: full `iconvdata` breadth and deterministic table-generation closure.
 
 ## Deterministic Stub Surface

@@ -0,0 +1,67 @@
+{
+  "schema_version": 1,
+  "bead": "bd-7cba",
+  "description": "Explicit phase-1 iconv codec scope/exclusion ledger with compatibility intent. This keeps user-facing claims bounded while deterministic table-generation work remains in progress.",
+  "phase": "phase1",
+  "included_codecs": [
+    {
+      "canonical": "UTF-8",
+      "aliases": ["utf8"],
+      "compatibility_intent": "Supported for strict+hardened fixtures and host-parity validation in iconv phase-1."
+    },
+    {
+      "canonical": "ISO-8859-1",
+      "aliases": ["latin1"],
+      "compatibility_intent": "Supported as the phase-1 single-byte baseline for deterministic conversion/error semantics."
+    },
+    {
+      "canonical": "UTF-16LE",
+      "aliases": ["utf16le"],
+      "compatibility_intent": "Supported for deterministic little-endian wide-char conversion coverage."
+    },
+    {
+      "canonical": "UTF-32",
+      "aliases": ["utf32"],
+      "compatibility_intent": "Supported with deterministic BOM emission and bounds/error behavior."
+    }
+  ],
+  "excluded_codec_families": [
+    {
+      "canonical": "ISO-2022-CN-EXT",
+      "reason": "Stateful escape-sequence codec family deferred until deterministic table generation/checksum provenance closure (`bd-13ya`).",
+      "compatibility_intent": "Remain unsupported in phase-1; `iconv_open` must return EINVAL."
+    },
